title: Cohniella brachyphylla
intro: Cohniella brachyphylla is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, classified within the Apocynaceae family. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Cohniella brachyphylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cohniella brachyphylla is commonly prepared as a tincture or infused in tea form, and is also used in the production of medicinal extracts and capsules.
